notes: [ctf.flag.spawn_setup | presentation] Flag entity spawn + model/skin/bbox setup  -  Base flag is static at the spawn origin (no DropToFloor settle on non-fixed spawns); dropped flags now DO settle (MoveType.Toss integrator, see ctf.flag.drop_on_death).; No glow trail (g_ctf_flag_glowtrails), no dynamic team light (g_ctf_dynamiclights); EF_LOWPRECISION is set, EF_FULLBRIGHT only when g_ctf_fullbrightflags (matches Base default off).

[ctf.flag.capture | presentation] Flag capture + caps/score/captime  -  Only the PERSISTENT capture-time record (ctf_CaptureRecord ServerProgsDB write + cross-session g_ctf_leaderboard) remains unported (needs a persistent DB). The in-match fastest-cap broadcast (CTF_CAPTURE_TIME/_BROKEN/_UNBROKEN, picked via MSG_CHOICE) IS now fired by CaptureRecord.; Per-flag score_capture override + cnt-group guard not modeled (own flag home requirement IS faithful).

[ctf.flag.fckill_score | presentation] Flag-carrier-kill score + damage/force factors + auto-helpme  -  Auto-helpme is modeled as a carrier-sprite helpme FLASH (GtHelpMeUntil networked via ServerNet, consumed in CollectWaypoints), not the full Base WaypointSprite_HelpMePing sprite; effective-HP test approximates healtharmor_maxdamage with health+armor.

[ctf.flag.remove_player | liveness] Carrier disconnect/observe/portal/vehicle drops flag  -  PORTAL-teleport carry-leave drop (g_ctf_portalteleport) is still NOT wired — the ctf PortalTeleport hook has no port dispatch (the PortalTeleport event itself is never fired in the port; BuffsMutator/NadeTranslocateBoom note it omitted). Disconnect + MakePlayerObserver (OnPlayerRemoved) and vehicle board/leave (OnVehicleEnter/Exit) DO drop the flag now.

[ctf.hud.waypoints | values,presentation] Flag waypoint sprites (base / dropped / carrier)  -  Base/dropped/carrier-own (health bar) + carrier-enemy (stalemate/self-carry reveal) sprites + the carrier helpme FLASH are emitted; no dedicated flag-return sprite, no on-pickup enemy-FC spotting reveal (g_ctf_flagcarrier_waypointforenemy_spotting).

[ctf.oneflag | liveness] One-flag CTF (neutral carriable flag)  -  Liveness depends on a map shipping item_flag_neutral; the neutral-pickup + team-base-capture + reverse logic is present but exercised only on a one-flag map. g_ctf_reverse (two-flag reverse) is NOT modeled (only OneFlagReverse).

[ctf.abort_speedrun | logic] Return carried flag on speedrun abort (AbortSpeedrun)  -  QC the ctf AbortSpeedrun hook: when a player aborts a speedrun (CTS/race-style speedrunning) while carrying a flag, the flag is force-respawned (ctf_RespawnFlag) and INFO_CTF_FLAGRETURN_ABORTRUN is broadcast. The port has no AbortSpeedrun dispatch (the speedrun/CTS abort path is not wired into CTF), so a carried flag is not auto-returned on a speedrun abort.

[ctf.drop_special_items | logic,liveness] Throw flag on DropSpecialItems  -  QC the ctf DropSpecialItems hook throws a carried flag (ctf_Handle_Throw DROP_THROW) when the generic DropSpecialItems event fires (e.g. nade-drop / forced item-drop). The port drops a carried flag on death (OnDeath -> DropFlag) but has no DropSpecialItems hook, so any non-death special-item-drop path that exists for other items does NOT also eject the flag.

[ctf.logdeath_itemcode | logic] Flag-carrier death-log item code ('F')  -  QC appends the item code 'F' to a player's death-log line when they die carrying the flag (LogDeath_AppendItemCodes). The port's death logging does not append the CTF flag item code.

[ctf.ignore_frags | logic] Suppress frag score in CTF (g_ctf_ignore_frags)  -  QC the ctf GiveFragsForKill hook zeroes the per-kill frag score (M_ARGV(2)=0) and, when g_ctf_ignore_frags is set, also suppresses the frag COUNT entirely. The port has no GiveFragsForKill hook for CTF, so a CTF kill still books a normal frag and g_ctf_ignore_frags is a no-op.

[ctf.leaderboard | logic,presentation] Persistent cross-session capture leaderboard (g_ctf_leaderboard)  -  QC the g_ctf_leaderboard subsystem (ClientConnect race_SendAll, GetPressedKeys race_checkAndWriteName, GetRecords) sends + records the persistent cross-session fastest-capture leaderboard via the race record store + ServerProgsDB. The port ports neither the race record store nor any of these CTF hooks, so the persistent leaderboard does not exist (the in-match fastest-cap broadcast in ctf.flag.capture is unaffected).

[ctf.followfc_superspec | logic] Superspectate 'follow flag carrier' command (followfc)  -  QC the ctf SV_ParseClientCommand hook handles the spectator 'followfc [team]' command (gated by g_superspectate): a superspectator jumps to spectating the/an active flag carrier (optionally of a given team), or gets a 'No active flag carrier' message. The port has no followfc command and no superspectate subsystem.

[ctf.prefer_score_clear | logic] Prefer player-score on clear (PreferPlayerScore_Clear)  -  QC the ctf PreferPlayerScore_Clear hook returns true, signalling the scoreboard-clear path to prefer the per-player score representation in CTF. The port has no equivalent hook; the port's scoreboard-clear path does not branch on a gametype preference, so this nuance is unmodeled.

[freezetag.mode.registration | values] Mode registration: teams, team-spawns, score/lead limits, FT scoring fields  -  Match limit semantics differ: Base reads mapinfo pointlimit (10) / leadlimit (6) via g_freezetag_point_limit=-1 passthrough; port keys off fraglimit/leadlimit. Live default round target (10) coincidentally matches, leadlimit 6 likely not honored.

[freezetag.unfreeze | presentation] Unfreeze: clear ice state, restore health, re-enable  -  Does not delete an ice entity (not spawned in the first place); the waypoint sprite IS cleared (it stops being collected once IsFrozen flips false).

[freezetag.revive.score | values] Revive scoring (reviver +1, FREEZETAG_REVIVALS +1; revived player return)  -  No nade revival bonus (g_nades_bonus_score_low) on a completed revive — minor, requires the nades mutator.

[freezetag.damage.softkill_void | logic,liveness] Frozen void/lava soft-kill teleport + g_frozen_damage_trigger; fall/nade revive  -  STILL OPEN — no soft-kill relocate path (g_frozen_damage_trigger 0): the NEEDKILL soft-kill (restore HP=1+armor, teleport to a spawn point, no weapon reset) in PlayerDies / the Damage_Calculate relocate branch is absent — FT.OnDamageCalculate leaves NEEDKILL deathtypes alone (frozen player dies in void/lava), and the relocate needs the server spawn selector (intentionally NOT done in the gametype layer). Base default cvar is 1 = die, so this branch is dormant at stock cvars regardless.; Fall-damage revive (g_frozen_revive_falldamage) is implemented and live (FreezeTag.cs:507-515): a hard enough DEATH_FALL hit thaws the player to falldamage_health (40) and sends FREEZETAG_REVIVED_FALL/_REVIVE_SELF. Default off, so dormant at stock cvars but correct when ena

[freezetag.round.flow | values] Round handler flow (warmup/countdown/round/end-delay, ROUND_OVER tie)  -  C# fallback timings (warmup 5/timelimit 180/enddelay 5) still differ from Base (10/360/0); masked live because gametypes-server.cfg ships. The round-timelimit-expiry tie path (CheckWinner ROUND_OVER) IS implemented (FreezeTag.cs:797-811: thaw all + ROUND_OVER notif + return -1) but depends on Handler.RoundEndTime being mirrored from the live RoundHandler.
[freezetag.win.roundlimit | values] Match win on round/lead limit (ST_FT_ROUNDS)  -  Lead-limit default (Base 6 via mapinfo) likely not honored (port reads leadlimit cvar, default 0 = off).

[freezetag.presentation.ice_model | logic,values,presentation,liveness] Ice entity model over frozen players (MDL_ICE, 20 frames, team color)  -  No distinct ice block entity (random 1-of-20 frame, team-colored, following the player at origin-16) — frozen players only get an icy-blue colormod on their normal model.

[freezetag.presentation.overlay_eventchase | presentation] Full-screen frozen overlay tint + cl_eventchase_frozen cam + damage HUD  -  Overlay + crosshair ring + eventchase cam are all driven on the host/listen-server path (LocalServerPlayer / its Frozen status effect); a pure remote client with no LocalServerPlayer shows none of them (the cross-client wepent/stat follow-up).

[freezetag.bots | logic,values,timing,liveness] Bot freeing / offense roles + frozen-target gating  -  Bots have no FT-specific freeing/offense roles — they won't deliberately route to and revive frozen teammates (havocbot_role_ft_freeing/offense + havocbot_goalrating_ft_freeplayers absent).; The frozen-target ATTACK gate (Bot_ForbidAttack) IS effectively covered: BotBrain.ShouldAttack (BotBrain.cs:793) skips a target carrying the shared Frozen status effect, so bots don't waste fire on frozen enemies. The dedicated FT Bot_ForbidAttack hook is not registered, but the generic frozen-skip yields the same outcome.

[freezetag.frozen.no_pickup | liveness] Frozen players can't pick up items or buffs (ItemTouch/BuffTouch)  -  BuffTouch has no separate hook in the port — OnItemTouch (the world-item gate) is the only frozen-pickup gate; buff-pickups route through a different path that has no frozen gate. Minor: frozen players can't initiate movement, and buffs are a mutator. World-item pickups ARE correctly gated.

[freezetag.frags.center_message | logic,presentation,liveness] Per-freeze choice centerprint ('You froze X' / 'You were frozen by Y')  -  Base ft FragCenterMessage replaces the generic kill centerprint with CHOICE_FRAG_FREEZE (attacker: 'You froze X', with kill count + ping) and CHOICE_FRAGGED_FREEZE (target: 'You were frozen by Y'), suppressing it entirely when the target was already frozen (pushed off a cliff). The choice notifications are DECLARED in NotificationsList.cs (1270-1271) but FreezeTag has no FragCenterMessage hook to emit them — they have no live producer. The kill-feed INFO lines (INFO_FREEZETAG_FREEZE/SELF) DO fire (covered by freezetag.presentation.notifications); only the per-kill CHOICE centerprint to the two involved players is missing.

[freezetag.presentation.frozen_anim | logic,presentation] Frozen-pose player animation (PlayerAnim ANIMSTATE_FROZEN)  -  Base ft PlayerAnim ORs ANIMSTATE_FROZEN into the player's animation state so a frozen player holds the static frozen pose. The port conveys frozen-ness only via the FrozenColormod tint (freezetag.presentation.ice_model); the frozen animation-state freeze is not applied, so a frozen player's normal animation may keep playing under the tint.

[freezetag.notif.alone | logic,presentation,liveness] Last-survivor 'You are now alone!' centerprint (LastPlayerForTeam → CENTER_ALONE)  -  Base freezetag_LastPlayerForTeam_Notify (called from PlayerDies/PlayerSpawn/teamchange when a freeze leaves exactly one unfrozen teammate alive) sends CENTER_ALONE to that survivor — the 'you are now alone' warning. The port's FreezeTag never calls a NotifyLastPlayerForTeam (the method exists only on ClanArena.cs, not shared, and FreezeTag.Freeze/OnDeath/OnPlayerSpawn don't invoke it). The ALONE center notification IS declared (NotificationsList.cs:850) but has no live FT producer.

[buffs.item.spawnfunc | presentation] Map buff pickup item (item_buff_<type> spawnfunc + compat classnames)  -  Per-buff visual (skin/color/glowmod) is NOT applied to the spawned item: ConfigureBuffEntity sets the relic model + size but never assigns buff.m_skin / buff.m_color / glowmod, so the pickup renders as the uncolored relic regardless of which buff it carries (QC buff_Think colors it). Presentation partial.; Minor lifecycle omissions vs buff_Init: PFLAGS_FULLDYNAMIC dynamic light + buff_Customize light pulse, bot_pickupbasevalue 1000 (bots don't weight buff items), and the buff_Waypoint_Spawn build-finished waypoint (see buffs.waypoint) are not ported.

[buffs.item.auto_spawn | logic,timing,presentation,liveness] Auto-spawn buffs when none exist (g_buffs_spawn_count)  -  Default count is 0 so this never runs unless explicitly configured (matches Base default) — this stays liveness:partial. (The map item_buff_* spawnfuncs are now the primary obtain path; see buffs.item.spawnfunc.); QC defers via InitializeEntity(buffs_DelayedInit, INITPRIO_FINDTARGET) and sets spawnflags|=64 (always randomize/relocate) on each seed; port sets BuffAlwaysRelocate=true on each auto-seed (SpawnInitialBuffs), then routes through ConfigureBuffEntity (which now sets the relic model + box + cooldown + think + touch, same as the map spawn). Still spawns immediately at Hook() (not via the INITPRIO_FINDTARGET defer). Residual presentation gap: per-buff skin/color/glowmod still not applied, and no waypoint.

[buffs.item.touch_pickup | presentation] Buff pickup + autoreplace on touch  -  EFFECT_ITEM_PICKUP particle is still not spawned (no effect-spawn service); only the pickup sound + notifications play.; cl_buffs_autoreplace is read as the server-side value (no per-client REPLICATE store), so it can't differ per client; functionally equivalent on a server-wide setting.

[buffs.item.respawn_cooldown | logic,timing,presentation] Buff item cooldown / respawn cycle (buff_Think + buff_SetCooldown)  -  Port respawn is still a single Think after cooldown_respawn (clears Owner and runs MaybeRandomize/MaybeRelocate); no buff_activetime countdown, no activate-cooldown arming on round start (Base buff_Reset uses g_buffs_cooldown_activate; port only models cooldown_respawn), no buff_active/buff_activetime state machine, no buff_Customize light pulse.; SND_STRENGTH_RESPAWN + EFFECT_ITEM_RESPAWN ARE now emitted on (re)activation (ActivateBuff:336-337, ScheduleRespawn:462-463). Still missing: the waypoint build-finished timer and g_buffs_pickup_anyway instant-respawn.; Owner-gone / dead / frozen / wrong-buff respawn conditions (QC buff_Think) are still collapsed: the port unconditionally clears the owner and re-arms after the respawn cooldown.

[buffs.item.random_location | logic,values,timing,presentation] Random location / lifetime / randomize-on-reset (buff_Respawn / buff_Reset)  -  MoveToRandomMapLocation relocation IS done (BallEntity.RandomMapLocation, which folds in the SelectSpawnPoint fallback), with the '0 0 200' launch + a mins*1.5/maxs*1.5 tracebox unstick + g_buffs_random_lifetime arming + SND_KA_RESPAWN on relocation. The g_buffs_randomize teamplay gate (& (teamplay?2:1)) and g_buffs_random_location/spawnflag-64 relocate gate are honored.; Still missing: g_buffs_random_location_attempts is not read (RandomMapLocation uses its own fixed sampling rather than the attempts/256/1024 box-content rejection of MoveToRandomMapLocation), so the relocation's content-validity check differs from QC. No EFFECT_ELECTRO_COMBO x2 visual at the relocation, no WaypointSprite_Ping.

[buffs.disability | logic,values,liveness] Disability buff — stun on hit (slow movement/attack/monsters)  -  Movement-speed slow (Stunned x0.7 via SpeedMultiplier, OnPlayerPhysics:678-680) is LIVE.; Weapon fire-time slow (WeaponRateFactor x1.5) is LIVE: OnWeaponRateFactor (691-697) multiplies args.Factor by 1.5 for a stunned player; WeaponRateFactor.Call has a live site (WeaponFireGate).; STILL MISSING: weapon projectile-speed slow (WeaponSpeedFactor x0.7) and monster run/walk slow (MonsterMove x0.7) — BuffsMutator subscribes neither hook (no WeaponSpeedFactor mutator-hook chain exists; MonsterMove is unsubscribed).

[buffs.inferno | presentation] Inferno buff — burn-on-hit + fire/lava resistance  -  Carrier-as-target fire-immunity (DEATH_FIRE -> 0) + half-lava (DEATH_LAVA x0.5) present and match Base. The 0.5 lava factor is QC's hardcoded `0.5` (QC has a `TODO cvarize` so this is faithful).; Burn-on-hit via MonsterFramework.AddFireDamage applies the shared Burning status effect rather than QC's .fire_* accumulator; the integrated total over the burn window approximates QC. Minor numeric divergence in the per-tick distribution, not the total or the curve.; Presentation: no EF_FLAME flame visual is networked specifically for inferno's burn.

[buffs.swapper | logic,presentation] Swapper buff — swap places with nearest enemy  -  Core swap is implemented and LIVE: nearest enemy within range (DIFF_TEAM/dead/frozen/vehicle filtered), origin/velocity/angles + oldorigin exchanged, SND_KA_RESPAWN at both ends, buff_RemoveAll consumes the buff. The ForbidThrowCurrentWeapon hook has live Call sites (WeaponThrowing.cs:149/185).; Divergences from QC: uses center-distance + Api.Entities.FindInRadius instead of WarpZone_FindRadius (no warpzone traversal); omits Drop_Special_Items(closest) (flag/key carriers keep their objective on swap); omits MUTATOR_CALLHOOK(PortalTeleport, player); omits the EFFECT_ELECTRO_COMBO x2 visual at both endpoints.

[buffs.carrier_model | logic,values,timing,presentation] Carrier buff_model glow  -  A player holding a buff still has no colored MDL_BUFF glow model attached: no buffs_BuffModel_Spawn/Update/Think/Customize, no removal on observer/disconnect. EF_NOSHADOW IS set on the player while held (ApplyBuff:461), but the glow model itself is absent.

[buffs.buff_effect | logic,values,timing,presentation] Carrier buff particle trail (buff_Effect / g_buffs_effects)  -  No per-carrier Send_Effect particle emission throttled by buff_effect_delay; g_buffs_effects (default ON) is never read. EntityMutatorState declares a buff_effect_delay field but nothing emits the effect.

[buffs.waypoint | logic,values,timing,presentation] Buff item waypoint sprite (WP_Buff radar/HUD)  -  No WP_Buff radar/HUD waypoint on buff items (no buff_Waypoint_Spawn, no RADARICON_Buff, no build-finished timer); the colored buff icon never appears on radar/screen edge, and there is no per-viewer hide-if-already-held logic.

[buffs.replace_powerups | presentation] Replace map powerups with buffs (FilterItem)  -  Replacement buff item inherits the spawnfunc presentation gap (buffs.item.spawnfunc): per-buff skin/color/glowmod is not applied, so the replaced powerup renders as the uncolored relic. No carrier glow/waypoint either.

[buffs.observer_cleanup | logic,presentation] Strip buff visuals on observer / disconnect  -  Still no MakePlayerObserver / ClientDisconnect handler to run buffs_RemovePlayer (delete the carrier buff_model). Moot only because the buff_model glow itself is unimplemented (buffs.carrier_model).

[minelayer.fire.lay_mine | liveness] Primary fire: lay a tossed mine  -  WEP_FLAG_MUTATORBLOCKED (Minelayer.cs:63): the weapon is excluded from the default loadout/allweapons (GiveItems.cs:340 = SpawnFlags & (MutatorBlocked|Hidden) != 0), so this fire path is NOT reached in a normal match. Live only when granted via weaponarena/NewToys/impulse-give. WrThink dispatch (WeaponFireDriver) is correct on that conditional path. Liveness stays partial.
[minelayer.fire.mine_limit | liveness] Per-player mine limit gate  -  Liveness partial: MUTATORBLOCKED weapon (see minelayer.fire.lay_mine).
[minelayer.mine.stick | liveness] Mine sticks to BSP surface and locks in place  -  Liveness partial: MUTATORBLOCKED weapon (see minelayer.fire.lay_mine).

[minelayer.mine.proximity | liveness] Proximity detonation (closer enemy = sooner)  -  Base proximity loop also skips IS_INDEPENDENT_PLAYER(head); the port does not model independent-player mode, so that exclusion is absent (no functional effect outside that unported mode).; Liveness partial: MUTATORBLOCKED weapon (see minelayer.fire.lay_mine).

[minelayer.mine.lifetime_countdown | liveness] Lifetime expiry + countdown warning  -  Port lifetime-arm branch adds an extra `self.Frame == 0f` term to the QC `!mine_time && cnt > 0` guard. Redundant (Frame is only set to 1 after MaxHealth is set, so MaxHealth==0f already excludes re-entry; deathTime is always >0 since lifetime-countdown=29.5) — no behavioral difference. Model note only.; Liveness partial: MUTATORBLOCKED weapon (see minelayer.fire.lay_mine).

[minelayer.mine.owner_death | liveness] Owner death/disconnect/frozen auto-detonate  -  HITTYPE_BOUNCE deathtype tag on the auto-detonate is carried only as a bool param (the port's int weapon-id deathtype has no bounce flag); the obituary picks the same splash murder string regardless, so this is documentation-only (minor).; Liveness partial: MUTATORBLOCKED weapon (see minelayer.fire.lay_mine).

[minelayer.fire.remote_detonate | liveness] Secondary fire: remote-detonate all placed mines  -  Port detonates inline in RemoteDetonate rather than flagging minelayer_detonate=true and letting each mine's own W_MineLayer_Think call W_MineLayer_RemoteExplode the next tick (one-tick timing difference; behaviorally equivalent in practice).; deathtype HITTYPE_BOUNCE tag on remote blasts not set (obituary picks the splash murder string regardless; inert).; Liveness partial: MUTATORBLOCKED weapon (see minelayer.fire.lay_mine).

[minelayer.mine.explode | liveness] Explosion: radius damage + knockback + fx  -  Airshot-achievement branch is ported (Explode:391-395 -> NotificationSystem.Announce('ACHIEVEMENT_AIRSHOT')) but UNREACHABLE for the mine in Base too: every W_MineLayer_Explode caller (Explode_think, ProximityExplode) passes directhitentity=NULL, so the QC `if (directhitentity.takedamage == DAMAGE_AIM ...)` guard never fires for a mine. Carried for structural fidelity. Not a gap (faithful to the unreachable Base branch).; Liveness partial: MUTATORBLOCKED weapon (see minelayer.fire.lay_mine).

[minelayer.mine.render | presentation,liveness] Placed-mine model + networking (visible mine)  -  No CSQCProjectile(PROJECTILE_MINE) in-flight networking analogue: Base networks the in-flight mine as a CSQC projectile and only the STUCK mine is a plain server entity. The port renders the mine as a plain server model for its whole life, so in-flight prediction/trail networking differs (functional but not the CSQC path).

[minelayer.ammo.checks | liveness] Ammo checks + reload  -  Port WrThink omits the forced-reload branch, but reload_ammo defaults to 0 so Base never enters it either — faithful at default values (would diverge if reload_ammo were set).; Base wr_checkammo1 also counts clip_load (weapon_load) as a second ammo source; port checks only the rocket resource. Inert at reload_ammo=0 (clip is never loaded), so no behavioral difference at defaults.; Liveness partial: MUTATORBLOCKED weapon (see minelayer.fire.lay_mine).

[minelayer.describe | presentation] In-menu weapon-guide prose (describe)  -  The MENUQC describe() weapon-guide prose (minelayer.qc:549-563 — the multi-paragraph in-menu weapon description shown in the Weapons guide page) has no port analogue. Several other weapons reference describe() only in comments (Electro.cs:98, Tuba.cs:63, Crylink.cs:160) but the port has no weapon-guide menu page, so this is a menu-only presentation gap (no gameplay effect).

[bot-ai.think.throttle | values] Per-bot think throttle + god mode + ping (bot_think)  -  Simulated bot ping/aim-latency (CS(this).ping, bot_aimlatency) is not modeled; bots have zero network latency, so their lead/reaction is slightly sharper than stock at low skill.

[bot-ai.aim.lead | logic,values] Projectile lead + ballistic arc (bot_shotlead / findtrajectorywithleading)  -  Ballistic aim is a closed-form gravity-drop approximation, not QC's 10-trace tracetoss search; lobbed-weapon aim (mortar/nade) is less accurate, especially with obstacles in the arc, and never traces the world to confirm the toss lands. (UNCHANGED.); Shot speed is read from g_balance_<wep>_primary_speed/gravity for the single ChosenWeapon (via the BotAimShotSpeed override hook, now used by Devastator/Crylink/Fireball), not per-weaponentity with W_WeaponSpeedFactor applied; weapons without those cvars are treated as hitscan (no lead). (PARTIALLY NARROWED.)

[bot-ai.target.chooseenemy | logic] Enemy selection (nearest attackable with LOS, sticky tracking, SUPERBOT rating)  -  No second see-through-transparent (DPCONTENTS_OPAQUE) scan pass and no misc_breakablemodel secondary targeting; bots won't shoot breakables or acquire targets through transparent surfaces. (UNCHANGED.)

[bot-ai.weapon.choose | logic,values] Weapon selection by range + combos (havocbot_chooseweapon)  -  Weapon picking is a hitscan-at-range / splash-up-close bucket choosing the highest-impulse owned weapon, not the QC ordered far/mid/close priority lists. (UNCHANGED.); No weapon-combo behaviour (bot_ai_weapon_combo): bots don't hold a weapon for a follow-up combo shot. (UNCHANGED.); No bot_rangepreference shift, so per-bot 'sniper vs spammer' weapon-range personalities are absent. (UNCHANGED.)

[bot-ai.weapon.wr_aim | logic] Per-weapon fire driver wr_aim (secondary fire / detonate / combo / charge)  -  Only the ChosenWeapon's wr_aim is consulted — there is no MAX_WEAPONSLOTS per-slot loop (Base runs wr_aim for every owned weapon slot each think). (UNCHANGED.); wr_aim is ported on 14 of Base's 18 wr_aim weapons; still missing: vortex (charge), seeker, hlac, blaster. (NARROWED 2026-06-27 second pass from 12/18; mortar grenademooth toggle + hagar 15%-secondary burst are now ported.); lastfiredweapon combo bookkeeping is not modeled; combos that depend on the previously-fired weapon don't trigger.

[bot-ai.roles.goalrating | logic,values] Roles + goal rating (havocbot_role_generic / objective roles, routerating)  -  Goal rating still uses straight-line inverse distance (f*rangebias/(rangebias+vlen(dist))) instead of the QC waypoint-cost (Dijkstra) field; bots can prefer a goal near in a straight line but far by path. (UNCHANGED.); rangebias is still hardcoded 2000 for every rate call (items/enemies/roam) instead of the per-rating-call bias QC uses. (UNCHANGED.); No separate _movingtarget (5.5s) strategy interval for goals that can move. (UNCHANGED.); enemyplayers aggression refinement: QC skill>3 nudges the t factor by Strength/Shield powerup timers (StatusEffects_gettime); the port has no status-effect-expiry accessor yet, so that refinement is deferred.

[bot-ai.move.steer | logic] Goal-stack steering + obstacle/step jump (havocbot_movetogoal)  -  QC's full movetogoal handling of teleport/ladder/box-volume waypoints, water swim-up, and the per-goal stop/turn logic is partially collapsed; complex waypoint traversal is less robust than stock. (UNCHANGED.); BotTracewalk (reachability test, separate from the brain danger check) still uses a hardcoded JumpStepHeight const = 48 rather than the live derivation; non-default physics doesn't adjust that one path. (Minor.)

[bot-ai.move.danger | logic] Danger look-ahead (lava/void/cliff/trigger_hurt) + evade  -  Danger evade is still brake-only: the QC lateral evadedanger vector (steer along the safe edge, distance scaled by skill) is collapsed into a reverse-velocity brake, so bots back straight off a ledge instead of sidestepping along it. (UNCHANGED.); Per-waypoint danger-cost ROUTING is inert: a Waypoint.Danger field exists and A* adds nb.Danger, but no botframe_updatedangerousobjects producer ever SETS Danger (always 0), so routing still does not avoid chronically dangerous waypoints — the consumer is dead. (UNCHANGED.)

[bot-ai.combat.dodge | logic,values,timing] Combat movement (projectile dodge + retreat)  -  14 of Base's 20 bot_dodge producer files are ported (all weapon projectiles + the PhaserTurret beam); the 6 still missing are monsters (golem/spider), the napalm nade, the grappling hook, and the generic sv_turrets producer, so dodge does not trigger for those hazards. (NARROWED 2026-06-27 second pass from 3 producers.); Non-SUPERBOT bots still only do the generic CombatMovement strafe-flip + HP-bias (an invented behaviour), not a per-projectile dodge — Base also restricts havocbot_dodge to SUPERBOT, so this matches stock, but the generic strafe is not a Base behaviour.
